title: MUBs from bent functions
abstract: This note contains a simple construction of complete sets of MUBs, using bent functions to write the new basis vectors as explicit linear combinations of the standard basis.
summary: This paper presents a simplified method for constructing complete sets of Mutually Unbiased Bases in prime power dimensions. By using bent functions, the author provides explicit formulas for the new basis vectors as linear combinations of the standard basis. This approach unifies and generalizes previous constructions for both odd and even prime power dimensions.
main_problem: The construction of complete sets of Mutually Unbiased Bases (MUBs) in N-dimensional complex Hilbert spaces, specifically for prime power dimensions N = p^n.
main_result: The paper provides explicit constructions for complete sets of MUBs using 'mubent' sets of functions, covering both odd characteristic (p > 2) and characteristic 2 (p = 2) cases.
method: The construction utilizes bent functions and 'mubent' sets to define new basis vectors as linear combinations of the standard basis, employing algebraic tools like trace maps, primitive roots of unity, and symmetric matrices over Z4.
model_or_system: The work is set in the mathematical framework of N-dimensional complex Hilbert spaces (CN), focusing on dimensions that are prime powers.
important_parameters: The dimension N = p^n, where p is a prime number.
main_assumptions: The construction relies on the existence of specific algebraic structures such as mubent sets and spread sets of matrices.
paper_structure: The paper is organized into a construction for odd characteristics, a modified construction for characteristic 2 using Z4-valued functions, and a discussion relating the results to spread sets and finite geometry.
why_interesting: MUBs are fundamental to quantum state tomography and quantum key distribution; providing explicit, simple constructions is highly relevant for designing quantum protocols and understanding the geometry of quantum information.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
